description_long: This object is a tall, cylindrical stone bollard with a classical architectural design, featuring a smooth shaft and ornate detailing near the top and bottom. The shaft is vertically elongated and rests on a square, blocky base that provides stability. The top of the bollard is capped with a flat, square slab, which slightly overhangs the shaft and is supported by a decorative ring molding. The surface appears weathered, with subtle variations in gray tones and faint signs of aging, suggesting it is made from stone or concrete. The overall appearance is reminiscent of ancient Roman or Greek columns, lending an air of formality and durability. There are no visible connectors, chains, or reflective markings, and the object is solid and robust, likely intended as a permanent fixture in urban environments.
description: A tall, cylindrical stone bollard with a square base and a flat, ornate cap, featuring classical column-like detailing and a weathered gray surface.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full cylindrical shaft, ornate top molding, and square base clearly.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, with the shaft, cap, and base all visible and symmetrical.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same classical detailing and proportions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the cylindrical shaft and square base, with no additional features.
attribute_cap_design: flat, square slab with decorative molding
